title: Senior Traffic Engineer
jd_text:
Senior Traffic Engineer Amey are a leading provider of full life-cycle engineering, operations and decarbonisation solutions, for transport infrastructure and complex facilities. Working for us, you’ll be delivering sustainable infrastructure solutions that enhance life and protect our shared future. Our people are driven by a set of strong values, based on safety, insight and collaboration. The OpportunityWe have a fantastic opportunity for a permanent Senior Traffic Engineer to join our Consulting Highways team. This is a hybrid role and with our office based in Manchester we welcome applications from across the area Amey and Trafford Council are working in partnership to deliver environmental and infrastructure services for Trafford. The One Trafford Partnership maintains and improves nearly 800 km of roads, 17,000 km of footways, 150 structures, 30,000 street columns and illuminated signs and 37 parks, in addition to collecting waste from 97,000 households. Innovative, new technology will be introduced across the services to deliver a more efficient approach for the Council and the residents of Trafford.The Trafford Highways Consulting account provides the local highway authority function on behalf of Trafford Council. It comprises approximately 40 engineers, technicians and support staff and is organised into four teams dealing with Traffic, Development Control & Drainage, Highway Maintenance and Highway Structures. The role We are seeking an experienced and motivated Senior Traffic Engineer to undertake design and provide technical support within the Traffic and Transportation design team. This is an excellent opportunity to assist the Principal Engineer working on a varied range of traffic management and public realm projects. Responsibilities include: Preparation of solutions at various design stages of a project; preparation of drawings, reports, specifications and estimatesOversee the design, development and implementation of traffic engineering solutions including Traffic Regulation Orders, speed limits, traffic calming measures, pedestrian crossings and minor improvement schemes Support and assist in planning, monitoring and reporting of project resources and finances, including all gateway controlsPrepare technical reports for both internal and external purposesProvide technical support including the Client, managing all stages.Undertake technical work checks and approvals of drawings, Health and Safety packages and communications programmes Resolve technical problems and issues to support the delivery of projects or design objectivesResolve enquiries from Councillors, the public and stakeholders to support the delivery of the contract, projects or design objectivesBuild and maintain excellent relationships with internal and external business partners, customers and clientsManage, monitor and report financial and resources stage assessments for Senior Management and ClientsPrepare and develop bid opportunities with partners and stakeholdersReporting back to DfT /TfGM and other Stakeholders, supporting programme development and bids Assist less experienced colleagues in ensuring compliance with pre-defined standardsAssist with continuous improvement in the services provided by the teamTake ownership of personal development in order to build professional capability What you will bring to us: HNC/BEng/MEng in Civil Engineering or similar and be working towards or have attained Chartered status of the Institution of Civil Engineers / Institution of Highways and Transportation or similar.Client focused approach to service deliveryDesign experience within a traffic engineering environmentKnowledge of relevant design standards including the Design Manual for Roads and Bridges and Traffic Signs Regulations and General DirectionsExperience of designing and implementing Traffic Regulation OrdersExperience of designing a variety of schemes; parking partiesA proven knowledge of Community Engagement and how it can be utilised in a Traffic & Transportation environmentKnowledge of relevant legislation including the Road Traffic Regulation Act; the Local Authorities Traffic Orders (Procedure) (England and Wales) Regulations; the Traffic Management Act; the Highways Act and the Highways (Road Humps) RegulationsAwareness of Road Safety Audit proceduresExperience in working on both Local Authority and Capital maintenance and /or developer-led schemes as part of a multi-disciplinary design teamKnowledge of traffic and / or highway design products, standards and protocols for scheme development and delivery Experience in policy and report writingExperience of developing Highway Capital programmesExperience of budget monitoring and project managementUnderstanding of street works permit schemesUnderstanding of CDM RegulationsExperience in developing safe systems of work and risk assessments What we can offer you:At Amey, we recognise that our biggest asset is our people. That’s why when you join us, we offer flexibility, career development, a choice of benefits and support that help you through all life’s ups and downs. It’s the reason why Investors in People put us among the top 1% of employers. Career Development –  Our dedicated progression framework identifies your career pathway and progress within the organisation, and with dedicated mentoring and support on hand to ensure you hit your career milestones. We’re huge advocates for professional development and offer a recognition bonus for those achieving professional qualifications. Work-life Balance –  Work-life balance and flexibility are key for our success. We empower our people to make choices that are right for them, with hybrid, part-time and flexible work patterns. And with a network of offices across the UK, we’re open to discussing working options that suit you. Wellbeing –  Health cash plan, 24 GP, support and assistance programmes, wellbeing ambassadors and Wellbeing Wednesday, dental vouchers EDI- At Amey we celebrate our people and all that they are. This is reflected in our Affinity Group networks, providing a community of support and connection, a safe space to share experiences, learn from one another and generate ideas – Women @ Amey, NeuroDiversity, Armed Forces, Multicultural Network, Pride, Diversability and Parents & Carers. As one of us, you can really be yourself, because your individuality is an asset. You’ll be stretched, but always supported. Well recognise your hard work and look after your well-being too. title: Founding AI Engineer
jd_text:
OverviewAt Upsolve AI, we’re building the AI-native BI platform from first principles. We’ve already built the foundation: a modern embedded analytics platform designed to work seamlessly with LLMs and agentic systems. Now, we’re going all in on building the next generation of agentic data analysis - think “Cursor for data analytics,” integrated directly into any BI and reporting stack and designed to be embedded into our customers’ products.We’re a Y Combinator-backed (W24) startup with strong funding and support from top-tier investors (General Catalyst, Samsung Next, Soma Capital, and founders of Airtable and Handshake).The Founding TeamKa Ling Wu, Co-founder & CEO, previously led product solution development at nPlan, a Series A AI & Construction tech startup, and before that spent four years at Palantir, where she spearheaded HyperAuto and launched Palantir’s Korea operation.Serguei Balanovich, Co-founder & CTO, spent seven years at Palantir pioneering early Supply Chain and Manufacturing work and productizing it into HyperAuto.Together, we bring over 15 years of experience in data engineering and analytics, taking Palantir’s HyperAuto from inception to launch, scaling it to more than 50 enterprise customers in under 2.5 years, and having it featured in Palantir’s DPO S-1. We graduated from Y Combinator in Winter 2024.The OpportunityThis role is a key hire for Upsolve AI today. We have doubled down on building our agentic data analysis platform, and the quality of our agent performance will determine the trajectory of the business.As our founding AI engineer, you’ll work directly with the CTO to design and ship real-world LLM/agent solutions. You’ll run experiments, evaluate outcomes, and set product direction based on what proves successful. You won’t just write code you’ll be the deciding voice for what we build, how we build it, and why.This is not a research role. It’s a high-impact product engineering role in forward-deployed style where your work ships into production and is used by customers daily.Your first 12 months at Upsolve AI will involve...Designing, prototyping, and deploying agentic LLM systems into production with real customer workloads.Running experiments to explore architectures, frameworks, and approaches - and driving product direction from their results.Building evaluation, observability, and improvement pipelines for agent quality and reliability.Integrating with our modern BI platform and customer-facing workflows.Establishing best practices for LLM/agent development, from tooling to performance monitoring.Embedding and collaborating directly with users and customers to validate what “good” looks like in production.What You Bring To Upsolve AI7+ years of engineering experience, including deep experience shipping production LLM systems.2+ years track record of building agentic or multi-step reasoning systems that work in complex, real-world environments.Strong command of modern AI/LLM tooling (LangChain, Mastra, DSPy, vector DBs, eval frameworks, observability, OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, fine-tuning).Strong engineering ability (TypeScript preferred).Experience with databases, data warehouses, data systems, data connections, and big data engineering/analytics.Product mindset: ability to make opinionated calls, weigh trade-offs, and push for what delivers real customer value.Comfort working in an early-stage environment: high ownership, ambiguity, and rapid iteration.What we value and what’s in it for you?At Upsolve AI, we operate like a high-performance sports team. jd_text:
🏷️ Title: Software Engineer👫 Department: Engineering💰 Salary: £60,000 - £80,000🎁 Benefits & Culture: Lottie’s careers page here, Lottie’s tech careers page here🎥 Meet our CTPO: Solving hard problems in care📈 Equity: A very generous EMI share offering🌴 Holiday: 26 days + bank holidays💗 Mission: Elevate later life for everybody and build the care sector of the future💻 Office Policy: Hybrid, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the office🌍 Office Location: London Bridge, London, UK🔍 The Opportunity:The care sector is being transformed by technology, and we're looking for a Software Engineer to build a key part of this transformation. You'll drive the implementation of features for Found OS, our next-generation SaaS platform that's revolutionising care home management. You'll be working on the system that helps care providers manage enquiries, track occupancy, handle billing, and deliver better care to residents across the UK.This is a hands-on role where you'll contribute to technical excellence while shipping fast. You'll work across our full stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, Kubernetes), and experiment extensively with AI. We're looking for someone who embodies "outcomes over output" - someone who cares deeply about solving real problems for care providers, not just writing code.💻 What does this role involve?Building high-impact features for FoundOS, collaborating closely with your cross functional teamWriting clean, well-tested code that ships daily - we optimise for sustainable throughput and move fast. Collaborating with Product and Design to contribute to feature decisions based on care provider needs and business outcomes. Experimenting with AI tools and integrations to enhance both care provider workflows and internal productivity. Contributing to our platform evolution and improving developer experience. Contributing to our engineering culture of continuous improvement. 💪 What problems will you be solving?Revolutionising care management with AI - You'll integrate AI to automate mundane tasks, provide intelligent transcriptions and offer predictive analysis, freeing up care providers to focus on what matters most - resident care. Building the operational backbone of care homes - you'll develop the core FoundOS platform, building robust modules for enquiry tracking, resident occupancy, and billing. This work creates the essential tools care homes depend on daily to run their business efficiently and deliver excellent care. Connecting the fragmented care ecosystem - You'll create seamless integrations with crucial accounting software (QuickBooks, Sage) and care planning systems. Your work will also feed FoundOS data into our marketplace, providing unique insights that help families find the right care for their loved ones. Informing strategic decisions with data - You'll build comprehensive reporting and analytics dashboards that give care home operators the insights they need to optimise their operations and improve resident outcomes. 👀 We think you’ll be a great fit if…You have 1-4 years of hands-on software engineering experience with strong TypeScript skills and full-stack development across modern web technologies. You're product-minded. You think about user outcomes, not just technical solutions. You can translate business requirements into elegant technical solutions. You write code that others love to work with - clean, tested, and maintainable. You believe in continuous improvement and leave code cleaner than you found it. You're obsessed with AI and actively experiment with LLMs, coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, …), and AI-driven productivity improvements. You understand both the potential and limitations of current AI tools. You thrive with autonomy but love collaborating. You are a self-starter who can see tasks to completion while contributing meaningfully to team discussions and code reviews. You ship fast and iterate. You prefer "good enough today" over "perfect next week" and optimise for learning speed over prediction accuracy. You're genuinely curious about eldercare and motivated by our mission to transform how families find and access care. You're eager to learn and stay current with technology trends, especially in AI and web development. Sound like you but not quite 100%? Apply anyway - we're looking for potential and passion, not perfect CV match!💔 This role isn’t for you if…Our mission to elevate later life care doesn’t resonate with you. You prefer working in isolation rather than as part of a collaborative, fast-paced team. You're uncomfortable with ambiguity or prefer detailed specifications over problem-solving from first principles. You haven't embraced AI tools in your development workflow or aren't curious about AI's potential. You prioritize perfect architecture over shipping working software to users. You're looking for a role where you can specialise in a specific area (ex: pure backend or pure frontend)🔢 Lottie’s Interview ProcessScreening interview led by Lottie’s Talent Acquisition LeadFirst round interview led by Hiring ManagerTechnical assessment interview with the Hiring Manager and one member of Lottie’s Senior Management TeamBehavioural interview with Hiring ManagerFinal interview with Will Donnelly (Lottie’s Co-Founder & CEO)🤩 Candidate Experience: All candidates will be informed of their application status.Pre-Screen & First Interview: You'll receive an email notification, but no specific feedback will be provided at this stage. Hiring Manager Interview: If you are unsuccessful after this interview, you will receive constructive feedback via email. Case Study: If you are not successful after the case study, you'll receive constructive feedback via email and be offered a debrief call with our Talent Lead. Final Interview: For final-stage candidates who are not offered the role, we will provide your feedback live via a phone call. 🚀 Meet LottieFounded in London in July 2021, Lottie is a fast growth HealthTech start-up aiming to build the world’s first SaaS enabled marketplace for care and with a goal of allowing families to seamlessly book care online in a similar manner to reserving a holiday stay via Airbnb or Expedia.Since launching in July 2021, Lottie has supported millions of care seekers find the right care for loved ones and has raised over £25m from leading venture capital funds including Accel, General Catalyst and Kindred, as well as from well known technology entrepreneurs including Mike Hudack (ex-CPTO of Deliveroo & Monzo) and Tom Blomfield (ex-CEO of Monzo Bank). More recently, Lottie was the acclaimed winner of LinkedIn and Startup.co.uk Top Startup awards.As of today, Lottie offers three core products and services, including:Lottie (B2B2C Marketplace) - Free service that helps families find the UK’s best care homes and home care services. Found by Lottie (B2B Vertical SaaS) - AI-native OS software supporting care providers to effectively manage their enquiries, occupancy and finances. Seniorcare by Lottie (B2B Vertical SaaS) - Employee benefit helping companies deliver outstanding eldercare support to their employees. 🤩 Why Lottie?Career Opportunity - A career-defining opportunity to help shape the future of our flagship marketplace and tackle complex challenges across the stack!Early Hire Benefits - Be an early hire at one of the UK’s fastest growing startups and benefit from fast career progression, as well as a generous stock options package. Mission - Tech for good that aims to build the care industry of the future and to positively impact millions of peoples lives. Care is one of the final remaining large consumer industries that is yet to be disrupted and positively revolutionised by technology. Global Opportunity & Scalability - Be part of a company that is solving a real tangible problem that every family around the world faces at some point in their life. The role sits within the Transport Platform, which is a key member of the wider Consumer Lending Business Unit. Our mission is to keep Britain moving by providing customer focused solutions to facilitate access to vehicles, maintenance, and related services including Digital account management. We are committed to supporting the Group and wider UK in driving forward the sustainability agenda. In pursuit of our mission, we are on a journey to transform our core technology architecture and the technical capabilities of our colleagues. We're investing heavily in simplifying and modernising our technology architecture by developing new digital journeys, enabled by microservices, and leveraging a new data ecosystem. All using modern frameworks and native Cloud capabilities. Whilst embedding DevOps and Agile working practices to enable feature teams to increase autonomy and effectiveness. As a DevOps Lead Engineer, you'll work in a team of engineers to design, develop and integrate software features to achieve their goals. Working within a Feature Team, across Labs alongside Engineering Leads, Product Owners and Quality Engineers to achieve technology outcomes in respect of engineering enablement. WHERE YOU'LL MAKE A DIFFERENCE You should feel excited by the prospect of making lasting positive impact through: Leading cross-functional engineering teams to deliver secure, scalable, and high-quality DevOps capabilities across cloud-native platforms. You'll apply technical leadership to drive outcomes and foster team autonomy. Designing and orchestrating CI/CD pipelines using tools such as Harness, Jenkins, GitHub, and Terraform, with a focus on federation patterns like overrides and config maps. You'll enable teams across the platform to self-serve and deploy with confidence. Engineering Kubernetes-native solutions across Public and Private cloud environments, including workload orchestration, Istio-based networking, and storage class management. You'll ensure platform resilience and scalability. Building secure and compliant DevOps workflows by integrating tools like SonarQube and AquaSec into automated pipelines. You'll enforce guardrails and embed quality assurance from the start. Automating manual processes and enabling self-service capabilities across teams. You'll champion an automation-first mindset and reduce operational overhead through robust pipeline design. Applying strong stakeholder management across complex platforms, balancing competing priorities and communicating effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences. Strategic planning and roadmap development, looking 6-12 months ahead to evolve DevOps capabilities in line with business needs. You'll schedule work and anticipate future demand. Fostering a culture of enablement, where DevOps capabilities are federated-not centralised-empowering teams to own and operate their delivery pipelines. Mentoring and collaborating across Communities of Practice, driving continuous improvement through shared learning, experimentation, and leadership. WHY LLOYDS BANKING GROUP? At Lloyds Banking Group, we're inspired by a clear purpose; to help Britain prosper. Across the Group, our colleagues are passionate about making a difference to customers, businesses, and communities. Like the modern Britain we serve, we're evolving. Investing billions in our people, data and tech to transform the way we meet the ever-changing needs of our 26 million customers. We're growing with purpose. Join us on our journey and you will too. We'll take your personal and professional development seriously and provide opportunity to shape the financial services of the future. WE ARE LOOKING FOR PEOPLE WHO HAVE: To be considered for and successful in this role, you will need to be able to demonstrate experience in: Providing technical leadership across cross-functional teams, delivering secure, scalable, and high-quality DevOps capabilities on cloud-native platforms. Designing and orchestrating CI/CD pipelines using tools such as Harness, Jenkins, GitHub, and Terraform, with a focus on federation patterns including overrides and config maps. Engineering Kubernetes-native solutions across GCP environments, including workload orchestration, Istio-based networking, and storage class management. Working with GCP platform services, including databases such as Spanner, BigQuery, and AlloyDB/CloudSQL, as well as GCS buckets and IAM permissions and controls. Embedding security and quality assurance into automated pipelines using tools like SonarQube and AquaSec to enforce guardrails and ensure compliance. Automating manual processes and enabling self-service capabilities across teams, with a strong automation-first mindset. Managing multiple stakeholders across complex platforms, balancing competing priorities and communicating effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences. Planning and prioritising DevOps capability roadmaps, looking 6-12 months ahead to align with strategic goals. Experience with Liquibase for database schema management and familiarity with Nexus Repository for artefact management would be advantageous. Exposure to observability platforms such as Dynatrace. Experience working with teams to investigate and define remediation plans as part of root cause analysis following high-impact incidents is desirable. Our focus is to ensure we are inclusive every day, building an organisation that reflects modern society and celebrates diversity in all its forms. We want our people to feel that they belong and can be their best, regardless of background, identity, or culture. We were one of the first major organisations to set goals on diversity in senior roles, create a menopause health package, and a dedicated Working with Cancer initiative. And it is why we especially welcome applications from under-represented groups. We are disability confident. So, if you would like reasonable adjustments to be made to our recruitment processes, just let us know WE ALSO OFFER A WIDE-RANGING BENEFITS PACKAGE, WHICH INCLUDES: A generous pension contribution of up to 15% An annual performance-related bonus Share schemes including free shares. Benefits you can adapt to your lifestyle, such as discounted shopping. 30 days' holiday, with bank holidays on top A range of wellbeing initiatives and generous parental leave policies Ready for a career where you can have a positive impact as you learn, grow, and thrive? Apply today and find out more! Join our journey! Our continued commitment to helping Britain prosper means that as a colleague you can make a difference to customers, businesses, and communities. Together we have a key role to play in shaping the bank of the future, whilst the scale and reach of our Group means you'll continue to have opportunities to learn, grow and develop. We're focused on creating a values-led culture, and our approach to inclusion and diversity means that we all have the opportunity to make a real difference, together. As part of the Group's commitments as a result of ring-fencing legislation, colleagues based in the Crown Dependencies are required to be exclusively dedicated to the non-ring-fenced bank and its subsidiaries. This means that colleagues who are based in the Crown Dependencies would not be able to undertake roles for the Ring-Fenced Bank from their existing location and would need to consider relocation when applying for roles.
